negative-numbers.name: Negative Numbers
negative-numbers.content:
What are negative numbers?

Negative numbers are any number below 0. At first, this idea might seem confusing, but they actually are really simple numbers.

Imagine you are facing forward. If you take 3 steps, now you are 3 steps ahead of your original position. Now if you take two steps back, you are now at position 1. This can be seen in the expression:

3 - 2 = 1

Other examples like this can be easily made. For example, you take 5 steps forward and 3 back. This is expressed like so:

5 - 3 = 2

And the final distance forward in steps is finally 2, as the math says.

But what happens when you take more steps back than you took forward? Is there a wall stopping you? (we're going to assume not) How do you know what is your position?

That's why we have negative numbers. Imagine you took 3 steps forward and 5 steps back. The expression that reflects this behaviour is:

3 - 5 = -2

This is an exact opposition to the other one, and you can compare by yourself.

5 - 3 = 23 - 5 = -2

As you can see, the 2 has a minus sign added to it. What does it mean? The -2 means the 2 steps back you are from you original position. Were it 2, it would be two steps forward. -2 then, means 2 steps back.

I really like using this meme to see how it works. It portrays Michael Jackson doing the moonwalk as the negative numbers. It will help us understand how to operate with negative numbers.

The Key Idea

To learn to understand substraction as a "turning around"

Cases

8 + 6

Pretty self-explanatory, so we will skip it.

8 - 6

Also self-explanatory, so we will also skip it.

8 + (-6)

We will start explaining this one before the one on the left because this one is easier. Imagine we take 8 steps forward, and then we go full Moonwalk mode and walk 6 steps more. The thing is, that the Moonwalk walks backward, so we would walk the 6 steps to the back instead of to the front. That is actually the same as just substracting it normally. It is important to instantly relate this:

a + (-b) = c

To:

a - b = c

Because they are always the same by definition. Let's give examples.

8 + (-6) = 28 - 6 = 2
13 + (-7) = 613 - 7 = 6
11 + (-3) = 811 - 3 = 8

8 - (-6)
